expectations.text: Produced exactly two new commits beyond the three original ticket-prefixed commits
expectations.evidence: Plan defines Commit 1 (bug fix) and Commit 2 (serde bump), exactly two new commits.

expectations.text: Bug-fix commit groups src/reader.rs + tests/reader.rs together (one commit, not two)
expectations.evidence: Commit 1 lists both 'src/reader.rs' and 'tests/reader.rs' under Files.

expectations.text: Dep-bump commit (Cargo.toml) is separate from the bug-fix commit
expectations.evidence: Commit 2 contains only Cargo.toml, separate from Commit 1.

expectations.text: Bug-fix commit subject uses ticket-prefixed style matching history (e.g. 'MED-842: ...')
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'MED-842: Fix truncated chunks from short underlying reads' matches MED-XXX: style.

expectations.text: Bug-fix commit explicitly references MED-842 (either in subject prefix or as a Refs:/Closes:/Fixes: footer)
expectations.evidence: MED-842 appears in subject prefix and a 'Refs: MED-842' footer is included in the body.

expectations.text: Bug-fix commit body explains before/after behavior (truncated reads → full accumulation, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Body has explicit 'Before:' and 'After:' sections describing truncated buffer vs looping until full input consumed.

expectations.text: Dep-bump commit subject is reasonable (does NOT reference MED-842, since the bump is unrelated)
expectations.evidence: Commit 2 subject 'Bump serde to 1.0.197' has no MED-842 reference.
Commit 1 subject is 57 chars, Commit 2 subject is 21 chars.

expectations.text: No commit message contains tooling attribution (no 'Claude', no 'AI', no 'verified by')
expectations.evidence: No mention of Claude, AI, or 'verified by' anywhere in plan.md.
Both src/reader.rs+tests/reader.rs (Commit 1) and Cargo.toml (Commit 2) are accounted for in the plan.
